blocks.content: It was the last day of suspension.
Shirone and his friends, who had studied at the inn until dawn, woke up long after morning had passed.
After teasing each other about their disheveled appearances, they took baths.
By the time they returned the books to the library and entered the school, lunchtime was over.
The campus was quiet during class hours, with cleaners sweeping the stone paths.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is it, our last day of 'vacation.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Vacation? Suspension is a vacation?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We still had fun, right? What's next?
Shirone turned toward the dormitory.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: First, I'll clean my room. Classes start tomorrow, so I should tidy up today.

blocks.speaker: Friend
blocks.content: You're so meticulous. Alright, we'll be at the research club. Come by if you're bored.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sure. See you later.
After parting with his friends, Shirone returned to the dormitory.
His room was a mess after neglecting it during the preparation period for the presentation.
Rolling up his sleeves, he started cleaning and finished by organizing his textbooks on the shelf.
An unexpected week-long suspension.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But he'd gained something from it—no, something far greater than what he'd learned at school so far.
Falling behind was inevitable, but he planned to focus on classes, even if it meant cutting back on research club activities.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Neide and Iruki must feel the same.
His friends' eyes had burned with academic fervor at the inn.
They now had a clear goal: advancing to Class Four before the semester ended.
At Alpheas Magic School, promotions were decided at the end of each semester based on grades, with the cutoff being a staggering 80 points or above in every subject.
Not an average of 80, but 80 or higher in every single subject—a terrifyingly high bar.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It wasn't impossible, but it was undeniably difficult for any student.
Everyone had weak subjects based on their aptitudes.
Yet the school's educational philosophy was firm: Master all fields of knowledge to a certain level before specializing.
Thus, a graduate of the magic school was expected to be proficient in the basics of all magic types.
This was why graduates were highly regarded in magical society.
Not all current sorcerers were graduates.
Some became apprentices to sorcerers, gained enlightenment, or learned magic through dark paths.
Their advantage was abundant field experience and combat-suited magic.
But compared to graduates, their understanding of magic was lacking, and their growth potential was limited.
Hiring decisions depended on the job, but graduates undeniably had an edge.
The school's 80-point standard reflected modern societal demands.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haah. So what? In my case, I need to raise my average by 30 points.
No matter what insights Shirone had gained, this was still school.
To advance, he needed an all-around score of 80 or higher.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: With his newfound framework, it wasn't impossible.
Strengthening his knowledge from the presentation and studying divine particles would boost his grades significantly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But even considering that, his chances of promotion were roughly 30%.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, that's amazing, isn't it? Advancing to Class Four in half a year with a 30% chance?
Shirone thought positively.
Wasn't that a challenge worth taking?
If Neide and Iruki advanced while he remained in Class Five… the thought alone was horrifying.
Moreover, entering Class Four would make him eligible to apply for the graduate class.
That way, he could keep his promise to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Gasp! Right! Amy!
He hadn't seen her since the presentation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Back then, he'd been too excited to even think of looking for her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, I should've at least said hello. I was so thoughtless.
Amy had been a supportive friend during his toughest challenges.
This time, he'd be the one to support her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hehe! She'll be surprised!
Shirone carried two cups of coffee to the graduate class building.
One for Amy, the other for Seriel.
The bittersweet memory of running coffee errands for Amy brought a wry smile to his face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Back then, I thought my world was ending.
What a strange connection it had been.
Who would've thought the delinquent girl he met in an alley at twelve would now be his friend?
Shirone climbed the hill to the graduate building.
The steel archway, nicknamed the Iron Gate, stood imposingly at the entrance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is the pinnacle of the magic school.
Passing under the structure symbolizing a sorcerer's spirit, Shirone's heart grew solemn.
When the sixth-period class ended, graduate students streamed out.
Shirone spotted Amy and Seriel chatting near the central entrance and called out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy! Amy!
The graduates turned their heads, but Amy and Seriel, still deep in conversation, didn't seem to hear.
Playfully, Shirone sneaked up behind them and held out the coffee.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ta-da! A surprise!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What? Who are you? Why are you giving us coffee?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sorry. Surprised? I didn't say anything to catch you off guard. How's graduate class?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What? Who do you think you are? And why are you using informal speech with me but honorifics with Seriel?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Are you from the advanced class? No matter how interested you are, this isn't the way to approach someone. And you should use honorifics with seniors. What's wrong with you? You're not some delinquent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Seriel Senpai, it's me, Shirone. Don't you remember? Amy, you really don't know me? You're joking, right?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why would I know you? If you're a student, go study. Don't annoy people when and where you shouldn't. Do I look like a pushover?
Amy grabbed Shirone by the collar.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He was quiet for a while, and now this. It's all because of that guy. Hanging out with weirdos and causing trouble…
As she muttered to herself, Amy paused.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Huh? That guy? Who?
She felt like there was someone connected to why the male students had backed off, but she couldn't remember.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No… this can't be.
Shirone shook off her grip.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No! Stop lying!
Ow.
Amy clutched her wrist, wincing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why are you pretending not to know me? What did I do wrong? If you hate me, just say so! This is just cruel!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hey! Get lost!
Seriel shoved Shirone.
Against her, he couldn't even resist and fell on his backside.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Why are you bullying my friend? And a senior, no less! What class are you in? Do you want to get in trouble?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Seriel Senpai…
Amy's best friend.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A top-ranked student with exceptional skills, yet one who cried over romance novels.
Shirone remembered.
He remembered how she'd cheered him on during his toughest challenges.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But now, she was a completely different person.
Her eyes burned with hostility, and her clenched lips were full of resentment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No! This is a lie!
Shirone scrambled to his feet and ran.
If he stayed another second, he'd lose his mind.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Don't come back! If you pull this stunt again, I'm reporting you to the teachers!
Seriel cupped her hands around her mouth, still frowning as she turned to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Ugh, what's wrong with him? He looks innocent but is so persistent. Hey, Amy, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Huh? Ah, I'm fine.
Lost in thought, Amy snapped back to reality.
But an odd discomfort remained.
Watching Shirone run away, she felt an inexplicable sense of longing.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who was that? He acted like he really knew me.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Probably just trying his luck. Guys like that are everywhere these days. Overestimating themselves.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You think?
Even as classmates, their different majors made it hard to find time to talk.
As Seriel entered the classroom, Amy followed.
But before stepping inside, she glanced back at where Shirone had run.
She stood there until the bell rang.
Shirone ran like a madman.
Passing through the Iron Gate, he reached the advanced class building as the bell rang and students filed into classrooms.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mark! Mark!
Mark, who'd been chatting with Maria, turned his head.
Shirone rushed over and grabbed him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mark! You know me, right? Who am I? Tell me!

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Uh… who are you?
Shirone's heart pounded wildly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's me. Me.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: That's what I'm asking. You have to tell me for me to know.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Me! Shirone! Class Five! We took exams together! You really don't know?

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: That's not it! Why are you acting like this?
What did he want him to do? Mark wanted to snap back, but with others watching, he couldn't.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Hey, class started. Why aren't you inside?
Ms. Siena walked down the hallway.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher! It's me, Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Ms. Siena
blocks.content: Shirone? There's no student by that name in the advanced class. Who are you?
Shirone ran to her like a lifeline.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher, you don't know me? It's Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Ms. Siena
blocks.content: Teacher, you don't know me? It's Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Ms. Siena
blocks.content: There must be some mistake. I haven't heard of any new students. Are you really from this school?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: S-sorry! My mistake!
Hey! Wait!
Ignoring Ms. Siena's voice, Shirone fled desperately.
Now he understood. What was happening.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Everyone had forgotten him.
Hic… sob…
He felt like a lost child. Weak and helpless.
The world seemed terrifying.
Entering the East Building, Shirone got lost three times before reaching the Supernatural Psychic Science Research Club.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: My friends will remember. Of course they will. Why wouldn't they?
Hadn't they joked around just hours ago? Even if the whole world forgot, they should remember.
Opening the door, he saw Neide and Iruki chatting.
They looked surprised when they saw him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Neide, Iruki, it's me. Shirone.
His friends blinked without answering.
The dam holding back Shirone's emotions broke, and his face twisted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why aren't you saying anything? You remember me, right? You haven't forgotten?

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Uh… who are you?
Tears welled in Shirone's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You really don't know me? We were just together. We studied together yesterday and the day before!
Pfft…
Air escaped Neide's lips.
Then he clutched his stomach and burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Pffahaha! Did you see that? That expression! It's priceless!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I saw it! I saw it! 'You, you guys…' Pffahaha! Shirone, what's wrong with you?
Shirone couldn't process the situation.
He couldn't tell if this was reality or a dream.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? You remember me?

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: What nonsense is this? Did you have a nightmare? Do you know how ridiculous your face looks right now? Pffahaha!
Shirone's tension dissolved.
The weight on his chest finally lifted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, damn it! Then you should've said something! Why were you pretending not to know me?
Shirone, too exhausted to scold them, slumped onto the floor and covered his face.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Sit down first. What's going on?
Sitting on the couch, Shirone passionately recounted what had happened.
At first, the two chuckled in disbelief, but their expressions turned serious when Ms. Siena was mentioned.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Hmm, even Ms. Siena didn't know you? She wouldn't play such a prank.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Is that even possible?

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: If it's mental magic, yes. Though manipulating the memories of the entire school is questionable.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's possible. I experienced it firsthand.
